reasoning_classification: First frame shows Dr. Kevin Huguet introduction slide and title card 'Laparoscopic Sigmoid Resection' from Minimally Invasive Institute of Surgery. Subsequent frames show actual surgical footage of sigmoidectomy with endoloop placement on an appendage structure. This is an instructional/demonstration video from an educational institution, not a scored trainee performance. While endoloop technique is demonstrated, the context is surgical education/tutorial rather than an isolated FLS task performance.

frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'Laparoscopic Sigmoid Resection' with Dr. Kevin Huguet, MD, MPH, FACS introduction
Laparoscopic view of abdominal cavity with pink/red tissue visible; orange appendage structures visible in upper abdomen
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Anatomical survey phase; multiple appendices apparent

frame_analyses.description: Grasper instrument visible on right side approaching target appendage; pink tissue and vessel detail visible
Endoscopic view with scissor instrument visible; dissection of appendage base beginning; red/pink mesoappendix tissue visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Mesoappendical dissection phase; clear field exposure

frame_analyses.description: White-colored ligating loop material visible in laparoscopic field; loop beginning to encircle target appendage
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop introduction and positioning around appendage base

frame_analyses.description: Loop clearly encircles appendage at marked site; proper positioning on base visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop fully positioned around target appendage

frame_analyses.description: Pusher instrument visible advancing loop; loop cinching beginning at marked level on appendage
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pusher activation and knot advancement phase

frame_analyses.description: Loop fully cinched at base of appendage; tight knot at marked site visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop secured; knot appearance tight and at appropriate location

frame_analyses.description: Scissor instrument positioned at loop tail; cutting about to occur
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final phase preparation; scissors ready to transect excess loop

frame_analyses.description: Loop tail cut; appendage fully ligated and transected from base
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion; appendage isolated from field

frame_analyses.description: Final view showing ligated appendage with intact loop at base; no slippage visible; clean closure

demonstrated_techniques: Loop introduction into surgical field with correct orientation
Positioning loop fully around appendage base at marked site
Pusher advancement for knot cinching with appropriate tension
Loop tail transection inside the trainer/abdomen
Anatomical identification and selective appendage targeting
verbal_cues: Video title indicates sigmoid resection context (routine colorectal endoscopic procedure)
common_errors_addressed: Video demonstrates avoidance of loop placement on wrong appendage by careful anatomical dissection
Shows proper depth control to place knot at marked base rather than on body of appendage
Demonstrates complete cinching to avoid loop slippage
Shows clean internal tail transection within cavity rather than external

scoring_rationale: This video is classified as instructional/educational demonstration content, not a trainee performance evaluation. Scoring FLS tasks is appropriate only for isolated task performances by trainees being evaluated. This footage is from an educational surgical video demonstrating endoloop technique within the context of a full sigmoidectomy procedure. No individual completion time, penalties against an isolated task, or trainee-specific performance metrics can be meaningfully extracted.
confidence_rationale: Clear educational framing with title slide and expert surgeon identifier. Footage shows complete, flawless endoloop execution integrated into live surgical procedure rather than isolated FLS task box setup. Cannot be scored as FLS task performance per rubric guidelines.
